Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 171. Reclaiming Runaway Servants (NL 018)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL EN-ía

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Bel-duri.

o 22

ARAD-ka mEN—BÀD

o 33

mDINGIR—mu-še-zib LÚv.NINDA

(3) The king installed Ilu-mušezib, a baker of Bel-emuranni, in Hatarikka. When the king passed on, Ku[baba]-ereš a (member) of his community and the (members) of the community of his relatives [made] him run away. They should make inquiries and return the man to me.

o 44

ša mEN—IGI-an-⸢ni⸣

o 55

LUGAL ina ⸢URU⸣.ḫa-ta-ri-ka

o 66

⸢ú-se⸣-[šib]-⸢šú⸣ :" a-ki

o 77

LUGAL [e-ti]-qu-ni

o 88

md⸢15⸣.[KÁ]—APIN-eš

o 99

ša ⸢kal⸣-[zi]-šú

o 1010

ša ⸢kal⸣-[zi] ⸢UN⸣-MEŠ-⸢šú⸣

o 1111

šu-ú-tú [ú]-⸢sa-aḫ⸣-[li]-⸢qu?⸣

o 1212

liš-ú-lu ⸢LÚ⸣ :"

o 1313

lu-sa-⸢ḫír⸣-u-ni

o 1414

a-ki [mu]-⸢ta⸣-nu

(14) When there was an [ep]idemic here, Ilu-bi'di a cohort [commander of the ... under] my jurisdiction killed a horse. I made up for the horse in his stead and he promised: "I'll give you the money." Now this Ilu-bi'di has died; his wife, as the king knows, has married a young boy and that person has been serving under me as a cook.

o 1515

a-na-⸢ka-ni⸣ :" m⸢DINGIR⸣—bi-iʾ-di

o 1616

⸢LÚv.GAL—ki⸣-ṣir ⸢x x⸣

o 1717

[ša] ŠU.2-ia

Reverse
r 18r 18

[a-na] ANŠE.KUR.RA

r 1919

i-du-ka

r 2020

ANŠE.KUR.RA [ina] ku-mu-šú

r 2121

ana-ku ú-sa-lim-me

r 2222

ma-a KUG.UD SUM-ka

r 2323

mDINGIR—bi-⸢iʾ⸣-di :" ÚŠ

r 2424

MÍ-šú LUGAL ⸢ú⸣-du :" DUMU? :" QÀL

r 2525

ta-ta-⸢ḫaz?⸣ [ina] ⸢LÚ.MU*⸣-MEŠ

r 2626

LÚ :" ⸢is⸣-si-ia :" i-du-la

r 2727

a-⸢ki LUGAL⸣ e-ti-qu-ni

(27) When the k[ing] had passed on, the (members) of the community came and made the man escape. His name is Tuqunu; let the king, my lord, ask [K]imumai and the members of the community whether he is a servant (of mine).

r 2828

ša ⸢kal-zi⸣ :" i-tal-ku-ni

r 2929

LÚ :" ⸢ú⸣-sa-aḫ-li-qu

r 3030

mLAL*-⸢nu?⸣ :" MU-šú

r 3131

a-⸢na⸣ [m]⸢ki?⸣-mu-ma-a.a

r 3232

⸢a⸣-na ša kal-zi

r 3333

LUGAL be-lí :" liš-ʾa-la

r 3434

šúm-mu la LÚv.ARAD

r 3535

šu-tu-ni

Edge
e. 1e. 1

mdPA—še-zib LÚv.A.⸢BA?⸣

(e. 1) Nabû-šezib, my scribe, is in the presence of Aššur-ereš; let the king, my lord, send him over.

e. 22

ina IGI maš-šur—KAM-eš :" LUGAL be-lí

e. 33

lu-še-bíl-šú

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P224447/.
